2026-03-26T03:50:06.0363180Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-03-26T03:50:06.0363625Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-03-26T03:50:06.0364011Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-03-26T03:50:06.0364516Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-03-26T03:50:06.0364746Z [36;1m[0m
2026-03-26T03:50:06.0364905Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-03-26T03:50:06.0365075Z [36;1m[0m
2026-03-26T03:50:06.0365246Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-03-26T03:50:06.0365472Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-03-26T03:50:06.0365667Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-03-26T03:50:06.0365955Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-03-26T03:50:06.0366204Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-03-26T03:53:36.3012767Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3014807Z Agent: 2. **Dynamic imports in README/snippets vs static imports in samples** — The README examples and `snippets.spec.ts` use `await import()` (dynamic imports), while the actual sample files (`pgConnection.ts`, `sequelizeConnection.ts`) use static ES module imports (`import { entraTokenProvider } from "@azure/postgresql-auth"`). 
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3016787Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3018646Z Agent: This inconsistency between the README examples and the samples is problematic—the dynamic import pattern used in snippets within async test blocks is unconventional and could confuse users about the proper way to import these modules.
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3020363Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3022579Z Agent: 3. **`GetEntraTokenPasswordOptions` interface not covered in README Key Concepts** — The README mentions `entraTokenProvider` but doesn't document that it accepts an options parameter `GetEntraTokenPasswordOptions` with a `scope` property, leaving users unaware they can customize the scope.

2026-03-26T03:53:36.3041105Z Agent: Now I have everything I need to make findings. Let me identify the key documentation issues:
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3041809Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3043844Z Agent: 1. **Snippet naming doesn't follow `ReadmeSample<Feature>` convention** (medium severity) — `snippets.spec.ts` uses `it("entraTokenProvider", ...)` etc. instead of `it("ReadmeSampleEntraTokenProvider", ...)`. Other packages use the `ReadmeSample<Feature>` naming.
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3045713Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3047619Z Agent: 2. **README code examples use dynamic `await import()` while samples use static imports** (medium severity) — The README examples use `const { entraTokenProvider } = await import("@azure/postgresql-auth")` which is an unusual dynamic import pattern. 
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3049262Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3050114Z Agent: The sample files use conventional static imports instead, so the README should align with this standard approach to match common usage patterns.
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3051137Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3052967Z Agent: 3. **`GetEntraTokenPasswordOptions` not described in README Key Concepts** (low severity) — The `scope` option (public API) is not mentioned in the README, leaving users unaware they can customize the OAuth scope.
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3054628Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3055721Z Agent: 4. **`SequelizeBeforeConnectHook` not mentioned in README** (low severity) — This exported interface has no mention in the README, although it's probably only needed by advanced users.
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3056499Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3056675Z Agent: 5. **Type alias**
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3056875Z Agent: 
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3058528Z Agent: There's a naming mismatch where `GetEntraAccessTokenOptions` is re-exported as `GetEntraTokenPasswordOptions`, and the internal name leaks into JSDoc comments in the source code, which could confuse users comparing source code against API docs. I'm also noticing that the `pool` variable in the test snippet is created but never used, which might trigger lint warnings though it's not strictly a documentation issue. Let me focus on the most impactful findings to surface.
2026-03-26T03:53:36.3060219Z Agent:

When the workflow is configured with `target: \"*\"`, you must specify `pull_request_number` to indicate which PR to target. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 10 review comment(s) can be created. Comments will be on the RIGHT side of the diff.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["path","line","body"],"properties":{"path":{"type":"string","description":"File path relative to the repository root (e.g., 'src/auth/login.js'). Must be a file that was changed in the PR."},"line":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"Line number for the comment. For single-line comments, this is the target line. For multi-line comments, this is the ending line."},"body":{"type":"string","description":"Review comment content in Markdown. Provide specific, actionable feedback about the code at this location."},"pull_request_number":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"Pull request number to add the review comment to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 876 in github.com/owner/repo/pull/876). If omitted, adds the comment to the PR that triggered this workflow. Required when the workflow target is '*' (any PR) — omitting it will cause the comment to fail."},"start_line":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"Starting line number for multi-line comments. When set, the comment spans from start_line to line. Omit for single-line comments."},"side":{"type":"string","enum":["LEFT","RIGHT"],"description":"Side of the diff to comment on: RIGHT for the new version (additions), LEFT for the old version (deletions). Defaults to RIGHT."},"repo":{"type":"string","description":"Target repository in 'owner/repo' format. If omitted, uses the configured target repository. Must be in the allowed-repos list if specified."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"submit_pull_request_review","description":"Submit a pull request review with a status decision. All create_pull_request_review_comment outputs are automatically collected and included as inline comments in this review. Use APPROVE to approve the PR, REQUEST_CHANGES to request changes, or COMMENT for general feedback without a decision. If you don't call this tool, review comments are still submitted as a COMMENT review. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 review(s) can be submitted.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","properties":{"body":{"type":"string","description":"Overall review summary in Markdown. Provide a high-level assessment of the changes. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["reason"],"properties":{"tool":{"type":"string","description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["message"],"properties":{"message":{"type":"string","description":"Status or completion message to log.
